name: the primitive type called oil

phase_0.boundary_condition: The periodic boundary conditions of the unit cell

VINIT.justification: The pre-topological state before any grid or lattice structure is defined.

TANCH.justification: The final boundary condition that wraps the lattice into a finite, closed manifold.

AFWD.justification: The forward movement from one lattice site to the next adjacent site.

AREV.justification: The return path from a site to its predecessor, maintaining lattice integrity.

CLINK.justification: The sequential application of translation vectors to reach distant nodes.

IMSCRIB.justification: The recognition of a single cell as the fundamental repeating unit of the oil topology.

FSPLIT.justification: The division of a single coordinate into distinct orthogonal axes of the lattice.

FFUSE.justification: The recombination of orthogonal components to identify a specific point in the bulk.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative state where a lattice node is correctly identified and occupied.

EVALF.justification: The negative state representing the space between defined lattice sites.

ENGAGR.justification: A state where a coordinate exists in a transition between two adjacent cells.

IFIX.justification: The permanent setting of the distance between nodes, defining the scale of the oil topology.

phase_3.both_description: a boundary point shared by adjacent cells

steps.domain_action: identify the fundamental unit cell as the self-referential base
fix the lattice constants to define the grid spacing
branch the position into orthogonal basis vectors
translate along the primary axis to the next node
confirm the arrival at a valid lattice site
recombine the axes to verify the global coordinate
chain multiple translations to form the extended lattice
branch to check for boundary conditions
identify a path that exits the local cell
apply inverse translation to wrap around the boundary
reconstitute the position within the periodic manifold
seal the lattice into a closed toroidal topology
